title: Run a Container as Non-Root
description:
## Drop Root in the Image

Running as root inside a container is a common, avoidable risk. A build context is prepared at **`/root/nrlab`** with this `Dockerfile`:

```dockerfile
FROM alpine:latest
RUN adduser -D appuser
USER appuser
CMD ["id", "-u"]
```

**Requirements:**
1. Build it and tag it **`nonroot:1.0`**
2. The image's default user must be **`appuser`** (so the container runs as a non-zero UID)

> 💡 `cd /root/nrlab && docker build -t nonroot:1.0 .` — then `docker run --rm nonroot:1.0` prints the UID (which must not be 0).

hints.title: Build the image
hints.body: The Dockerfile already creates a user and sets USER; you just need to build it with the right tag.

hints.title: Confirm the effective user
hints.body: Running the image prints its UID; a non-root user has a UID other than 0.

validation.description: Checks that nonroot:1.0 defaults to the appuser user and runs as a non-zero UID.

commands.description: Container runs as a non-root (non-zero) UID
